canonical_definition: IndustryOR is characterized in the provided literature as an open-source dataset of commercial Operations Research problems. One paper specifies its composition as a benchmark of "100 problems spanning linear, integer, and mixed-integer programming at various difficulty levels." The dataset's application appears to vary across studies. For instance, one line of work uses IndustryOR as the basis for developing a new benchmark focused on explainable Operations Research. In contrast, another study employs it directly as a benchmark to measure the construct of `Faithfulness`. Thus, while its core content is consistently described as a collection of real-world optimization problems, it is used both as a foundational dataset and as a direct evaluation instrument.
papers: Decision Information Meets Large Language Models: The Future of Explainable Operations Research
LLMOPT: Learning to Define and Solve General Optimization Problems from Scratch
Autoformulation of Mathematical Optimization Models Using LLMs
EquivaMap: Leveraging LLMs for Automatic Equivalence Checking of Optimization Formulations
OptMATH: A Scalable Bidirectional Data Synthesis Framework for Optimization Modeling

definitions.text: An open-source dataset of commercial Operations Research problems, used as a basis for creating a new benchmark for explainable OR.
